title: Millbrook Community Facebook Group: Water Testing Discussion
content: Thread: Lead in our water — what's really going on? Sarah M: 'Just saw the Watchdog article saying 150 ppb. That's terrifying! I have a toddler.' Reply — Mike D: 'I actually read the EPA report. It says 18 ppb, not 150. Still above the limit but way less scary than what the Watchdog is saying.' Reply — Sarah M: 'Are you sure? The Watchdog article seemed very detailed.' Reply — James T (City Water Dir.): 'The official EPA reading is 18 ppb at the 90th percentile. We are taking this seriously with treatment optimization. The 150 figure circulating online is incorrect.' Reply — Karen P: 'Even 18 is above the limit! The city should have fixed this years ago.' Reply — Mike D: 'Agreed it needs fixing, but let's be accurate about the numbers. 18 vs 150 is a huge difference.'
snippet: Community debates actual lead levels after conflicting reports — 18 ppb vs 150 ppb.
